title: Standalone compare CLI rejects the advertised threshold argument
reasoning: The usage string advertises an optional threshold, and the code below tries to read sys.argv[3], but the argument count guard requires exactly three argv entries. Running compare_benchmarks.py with current, baseline, and threshold exits with usage instead of honoring the argument.
recommendation: Change the guard to accept either two or three user arguments, for example len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4), then parse sys.argv[3] only when present.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked CLI tests cover the optional threshold path.
suggestedRegressionTest: Invoke the script entrypoint with current file, baseline file, and a threshold argument and assert it reaches compare_benchmarks instead of exiting with usage.
